policy_statement: The Government has set a Green Energy Target to improve the energy performance of the Government by 6% by 2024-25.
verbatim_text: We have also set  a  new  Green  Energy  Target  to  further improve the energy performance of the whole Government by 6% by 2024-25.
extraction_rationale: Quantified government operational energy target with a deadline.
reasoning: This is an outcome-oriented commitment: improving “energy performance… by 6%” with a clear deadline “by 2024-25,” and it applies to “the whole Government,” which provides scope. However, the phrasing “set a… Target” indicates intent/goal-setting rather than an explicitly binding requirement or enforceable mechanism in the provided text.
validation_reasoning: Quantifiable target: Yes — states "improve the energy performance of the whole Government by 6%" (measurable % outcome). Timeline: Yes — explicitly "by 2024-25". Binding mechanism: No — text only says "set a new Green Energy Target" and does not cite a mandate, regulation, or enforceable requirement. Spatial/scope specificity: Yes — applies to "the whole Government" (defines the covered entity/scope). Weak language: No — does not use 'encourage/explore/aim'; it asserts a set target. Strong language: Yes — includes an outcome with a hard number and deadline ("by 6% by 2024-25"). STRICT VALID RULE: Passes because it has both timeline and quantifiable target, and weak language is not detected (binding mechanism not required in that case).

policy_statement: WSD targets reducing average fresh water per capita consumption by 10% in 2030 (base year 2016) and containing fresh water demand at around 1 billion m³ per annum before 2040, via water conservation, water loss management, and expanded use of seawater/recycled water for non-potable purposes.
verbatim_text: To  achieve  the  goal  of  reducing  the  average fresh water per capita consumption by 10% in 2030,  using  2016  as  the  base  year,  and containing the fresh water demand at the level of around 1 billion m³   per annum before 2040, WSD  is  taking  forward  three  major  water demand management initiatives, namely water  conservation,  water  loss  management and expansion of the use of lower grade water (viz. seawater and recycled water) for non-potable purposes.
section_header: Combating Extreme Droughts and Safeguarding Water Supply
extraction_rationale: Quantified adaptation/water management targets with timeframes and named initiative pillars.
reasoning: The verbatim text contains two explicit, measurable outcome targets (10% per-capita freshwater reduction; ~1 billion m³/year demand cap) and two explicit time references (2030 and before 2040), tied to concrete implementation pillars (“taking forward three major… initiatives”). The language is goal/achievement-oriented (“achieve… reducing… containing… is taking forward”), and it is specific to WSD’s freshwater demand management scope (implicitly jurisdiction-wide under WSD). However, the excerpt does not clearly establish a legally binding mechanism (e.g., ordinance/mandate), so validity rests on the presence of quantifiable targets + timelines and the absence of purely weak/aspirational wording.
validation_reasoning: Quantifiable targets: (1) "reducing the average fresh water per capita consumption by 10%" and (2) "containing the fresh water demand at the level of around 1 billion m³ per annum". Timeline: "by 10% in 2030, using 2016 as the base year" and "before 2040". Auditable commitment/deliverable: the statement specifies outcome metrics (%, m³/yr) rather than only a study/process, and links them to implementation pillars: "WSD is taking forward three major water demand management initiatives" (water conservation, water loss management, expansion of lower grade water). Binding mechanism: no explicit enforceable instrument is cited (no law/mandate/ordinance), so marked No. Spatial specificity: no explicit location term (e.g., citywide/territory-wide) is stated in the excerpt; WSD is implied but not explicit, so marked No. Weak language: not primarily framed as 'encourage/explore/aim' without targets; it uses achievement framing with numeric outcomes. Strong language: "To achieve the goal of reducing..." and "containing..." with hard numbers and dates indicates strong target-setting. STRICT VALID RULE: passes because has_timeline=Yes and has_quantifiable_target=Yes, and weak_language_detected=No.

policy_statement: The Tseung Kwan O Desalination Plant (first stage) is targeted to be commissioned in 2023 with capacity about 135,000 m³/day (about 5% of overall fresh water demand) and provision to expand to 270,000 m³/day.
verbatim_text:
WSD is constructing the first stage of Tseung  Kwan  O  Desalination  Plant

The plant, targeted to be commissioned in 2023... It will have a daily capacity of  about  135  000  m³ ... with provision for future expansion to a daily capacity of up to 270 000 m³ .
extraction_rationale: Specific infrastructure project with commissioning date and capacity figures.
reasoning: The statement describes a concrete infrastructure deliverable (construction/commissioning of a desalination plant) with an explicit timeline (2023) and measurable capacity targets (135,000 m³/day with provision to expand to 270,000 m³/day). The verbatim text uses strong action language (“is constructing”) and specifies the location/project (“Tseung Kwan O Desalination Plant”), making it auditable. It does not explicitly describe a legal mandate/ordinance, but it is still a measurable, time-bound commitment.
validation_reasoning: Quantifiable target: daily capacity is specified as "about 135 000 m³" and "provision for future expansion to a daily capacity of up to 270 000 m³". Timeline: "targeted to be commissioned in 2023" provides an explicit year. Binding mechanism: no ordinance/requirement language is cited; it states agency action ("WSD is constructing") but not an enforceable mandate, so marked No. Spatial specificity: applies to a named facility/location: "Tseung Kwan O Desalination Plant". Weak language: not primarily framed as encourage/aim/explore; it states construction and a commissioning target, so No. Strong language: "WSD is constructing" plus hard numbers and a commissioning year indicates strong, auditable commitment, so Yes. STRICT VALID RULE: passes because it has both timeline=Yes and quantifiable_target=Yes, and weak_language=No (binding mechanism not required in that case).
